quick_facts.label: Typical appointment status
quick_facts.value: State-government JE is commonly a technical, non-gazetted post, but its group/classification differs by state and department. Maharashtra PWD’s 2023 rules classify JE (Civil) as Group B, Non-Gazetted; Puducherry PWD’s notified rules likewise list the post as Group B, non-gazetted and non-ministerial.

quick_facts.label: Core work
quick_facts.value: The role is field-facing project execution: preparing engineering data, site plans and estimates; supervising work against approved drawings and specifications; recording measurements; preparing bills; maintaining works/material records; and submitting reports or returns to superiors.

quick_facts.label: Direct-entry qualification
quick_facts.value: A diploma or degree in the relevant engineering branch is a common eligibility pattern, but the exact branch, recognition requirement, experience, age limits, domicile/language conditions and reservations must be checked in the specific state advertisement. For example, Maharashtra PWD specifies a diploma, degree or higher qualification in Civil Engineering; Puducherry PWD specifies a Civil Engineering diploma or bachelor’s degree.

quick_facts.label: Recruitment routes
quick_facts.value: Recruitment can include a competitive examination/direct nomination route and departmental promotion. Maharashtra PWD’s 2023 JE (Civil) rules provide both promotion and nomination through a competitive examination, in a stated 60:40 promotion-to-nomination ratio for that cadre; this is an example, not an all-India rule.

quick_facts.label: Mobility and posting
quick_facts.value: JE work may involve office, section and construction-site duties, and service rules can make an appointee transferable across the state. Maharashtra’s PWD JE (Civil) rules explicitly make appointees liable for transfer anywhere in Maharashtra.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: Pay is state- and cadre-specific rather than nationally uniform. As dated official examples, Puducherry PWD’s JE (Civil) rules list Level 6, ₹35,400–₹1,12,400, while a November 2025 West Bengal PWD appointment notification lists JE (Civil) in WBS ROPA 2019 Pay Matrix Level 12 with admissible allowances.

sections.heading: Responsibilities: execute public works with technical and record-keeping accountability
paragraphs: A state-government Junior Engineer generally serves as the section-level technical executor for public infrastructure and assets. Official departmental job descriptions show responsibility for gathering engineering data; producing site plans, rough drawings and estimates; laying out and supervising works undertaken by contractors or departmental labour; and ensuring work conforms to approved specifications, drawings, standards and samples.

The role also carries documentation and measurement responsibilities. Typical functions include recording work measurements, preparing measurement abstracts and bills, verifying bills, keeping material/tool and prescribed works registers, and sending progress reports and returns to senior engineering officers. In practice, the exact financial powers and approval authority remain defined by the relevant state’s PWD/irrigation/municipal rules and delegation orders.
bullets: Supervise construction, repair and maintenance work in an allotted section or project area.
Check workmanship and quality against approved drawings, specifications and standards.
Prepare or support estimates, drawings, site plans, quantities, measurement records and work bills.
Maintain technical, stock/material, testing and site-order records; escalate deviations, delays and site issues.
Coordinate with contractors, departmental labour, field staff and senior engineers.

sections.heading: Entry routes: state-specific competitive recruitment and internal promotion
paragraphs: The main external route is to apply against a state department, state recruitment board or public service commission notification and qualify the stated selection process. State JE posts are not governed by one universal national eligibility rule: the recruiting authority sets the discipline, qualification, age, category relaxations, language/computer requirements, examination scheme, document verification and other conditions in the advertisement.

Engineering diploma holders and degree holders can both be eligible in many Civil JE cadres. Maharashtra PWD’s 2023 rules, for instance, require at least a diploma or degree in Civil Engineering for nomination through the competitive-examination route. Puducherry PWD’s JE (Civil) rules similarly accept a Civil Engineering diploma or bachelor’s degree. Electrical and mechanical JE vacancies must be read separately because their required branch and technical syllabus may differ.

Internal progression can also be an entry route into the JE grade. In the Maharashtra PWD example, a Civil Engineering Assistant with at least seven years of regular continuous service and the prescribed professional examination may be considered for promotion; the same rules state that appointment is through promotion and nomination in a 60:40 ratio. These conditions are cadre-specific and should not be generalized to another state.
bullets: Match the engineering branch named in the notification—commonly Civil, Electrical or Mechanical.
Use only the official notification to confirm recognition of the diploma/degree, age cut-off date, reservation rules and selection stages.
For serving employees, check the departmental recruitment/promotion rules, seniority requirements and mandatory professional or language examinations.
Do not treat SSC JE, RRB JE or another state’s JE examination as appointment to a state-government JE post; they are separate recruitment channels and employers.

sections.heading: Skills: applied engineering, site control and accurate public-works records
paragraphs: The work calls for practical command of the relevant engineering discipline and the ability to interpret drawings, specifications, estimates and site conditions. Because the JE is responsible for supervision, measurements, bills and registers, accuracy in quantity measurement, documentation and compliance is as important as technical knowledge.

Public-works practice also requires quality awareness, contractor and labour coordination, reporting discipline and comfort with field inspections. For electrical JE work, official PWD guidance additionally emphasizes defect rectification, safe maintenance, periodic testing and recording test readings.
bullets: Engineering drawing and specification reading; surveying, estimation and quantity/measurement work.
Construction or maintenance supervision, inspection and quality-control awareness.
Measurement-book, bill, material/stock and site-record accuracy.
Clear reporting and coordination with contractors, field staff and Assistant/Executive Engineers.
For electrical/mechanical cadres: applicable safety, maintenance and testing practices.

sections.heading: Workplace: a mix of field sections, project sites and engineering offices
paragraphs: A JE is typically attached to a department or local engineering unit responsible for public assets—for example, public works, roads/buildings, irrigation, water supply, municipal works, electrical installations or mechanical services. The job combines on-site inspection and supervision with office-based estimating, records, bills and reporting.

Location stability should not be assumed. In Maharashtra PWD’s JE (Civil) rules, the appointee is liable for transfer anywhere in the state. Actual posting, transfer policy, site exposure, travel needs and emergency/maintenance duties vary by department, cadre and state.
bullets: Potential work settings: road/building works, irrigation or water assets, public facilities, electrical installations, workshops or local-body infrastructure.
Regular interaction with contractors, departmental labour, suppliers, residents/users and senior engineering staff is common.
Field visits are integral where work execution, measurements, inspections or maintenance are assigned.

sections.heading: Progression: departmental examinations, seniority/eligibility processes and Assistant Engineer opportunities
paragraphs: Career progression is governed by the state’s service and promotion rules, vacancies, seniority, qualifying service, performance/fitness and any mandatory examinations. A common engineering ladder includes eligibility for promotion to Assistant Engineer, but the time requirement and whether a degree, diploma, departmental examination or other condition is required differ among cadres.

Official evidence shows this pathway operates in state departments: Himachal Pradesh PWD publishes promotion material for JE to Assistant Engineer, and Uttarakhand’s public-service materials list promotion from Junior/Additional Assistant Engineer to Assistant Engineer in PWD and other engineering departments. Maharashtra’s JE rules also make passing prescribed professional, computer-operation and language examinations consequential for increments or promotion where applicable.
bullets: Build a reliable record in execution, measurement, quality, documentation and departmental procedures.
Track service-rule changes and departmental examination requirements from the employing department.
Check whether the target Assistant Engineer cadre differentiates between diploma and degree holders or prescribes a minimum regular-service period.
Promotion is not automatic: it depends on applicable rules, vacancies and formal selection/promotion processes.

sections.heading: Compensation context: pay matrices and allowances vary by state and cadre
paragraphs: There is no single India-wide state-government JE salary. The applicable basic pay comes from the recruiting state’s pay matrix and the particular departmental/cadre notification; admissible allowances and take-home pay depend on that state’s current pay rules, posting location, deductions and employment terms.

For dated comparison only, Puducherry PWD’s notified JE (Civil) recruitment rules show Pay Matrix Level 6, ₹35,400–₹1,12,400. A West Bengal PWD appointment notification dated November 25, 2025 places JE (Civil) in WBS ROPA 2019 Pay Matrix Level 12 and states that other allowances are admissible. These examples demonstrate variation and are not a salary forecast for a different state or future recruitment.
bullets: Verify basic pay, pay level, allowances, probation, pension/NPS coverage and contractual-versus-regular status in the specific advertisement and appointment rules.
Treat old grade-pay figures and coaching-site salary claims cautiously; use the current state notification and pay-revision orders.
Compare posts within the same state only after checking their cadre classification and current pay-matrix entry.
